description: Creates a line on a 2D graph as an infinite line, finite segment, or ray between two points. Supports color and dashed styling. Points are point() expressions or point variables.
utility: Draw a line, segment, or ray between two points

arguments.name: P1
arguments.description: First point (a point() expression or point variable)

arguments.name: P2
arguments.description: Second point (a point() expression or point variable)

arguments.name: lineType
arguments.description: Line type: line (infinite, default), segment (finite), ray (half-infinite)

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Color, dashed style, or stroke width

alternates.description: Line extending infinitely through both points
Finite segment from P1 to P2

alternates.variant: Ray from first through second point
alternates.description: Half-line starting at P1, passing through P2

examples.scenario: Line segment as diameter between two points with color (circle from a diameter lesson)
Line between two inline points for linear programming constraint
Deferred line through axis points (axis intercepts lesson)
Line segment between two inline points for triangle construction (two-tables lesson)
Infinite line through two inline points extending across graph
